title: allow Write(**) is a silent no-op -> the write is never pre-approved and stays ask

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P3-write-glob-asymmetry-DANGER/a-allow-starstar-noop/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The write was blocked by the system's permission mechanism. The harness is denying the operation to avoid side effects and recording an ask for permission.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P3-write-glob-asymmetry-DANGER/a-allow-starstar-noop/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The operation was denied by the system's permission controls. The Write tool cannot complete without explicit permission due to potential side effects.

hypothesis: allow Write(**) does NOT pre-approve the Write tool: the gitignore-style ** used for Read/Edit path rules is a silent no-op for the Write specifier (v2.1.201). With no allow matching, the write falls back to default ask — uniformly for a cwd-level file and a subdir file (the ** that 'should' cover everything covers nothing). Contrast the WORKING forms: Write(*) / bare Write (P2-a, pre-approved) and the relative directory-doublestar Write(<dir>/**) (S9-a, an ASK gate). The no-op forms are bare Write(**), exact/full path, single-star dir Write(dir/*), absolute-path forms, and ~ forms (P3-b/c/d).
